input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's the approach:

### 1. Define each theme as a `ResourceDictionary`

```xml
<!-- Themes/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="AccentColor">#0078D4</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

Create matching `DarkTheme.xaml` and `HighContrastTheme.xaml` with different color values but **the same keys**.

### 2. Load a default theme in `App.xaml`

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <ResourceDictionary>
        <ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
            <local:LightTheme />
        </ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
    </ResourceDictionary>
</Application.Resources>
```

### 3. Create a theme-switching helper

```csharp
public static class ThemeManager
{
    public static void ApplyTheme(string themeName)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
        mergedDicts.Clear();

        mergedDicts.Add(themeName switch
        {
            "Dark"         => new DarkTheme(),
            "HighContrast"  => new HighContrastTheme(),
            _              => new LightTheme(),
        });

        // Persist the choice
        Preferences.Set("AppTheme", themeName);
    }

    public static void LoadSavedTheme()
    {
        var saved = Preferences.Get("AppTheme", "Light");
        ApplyTheme(saved);
    }
}
```

Call `ThemeManager.LoadSavedTheme()` in your `App` constructor (after `InitializeComponent`).

### 4. Use `DynamicResource` everywhere

```xml
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource PageBackground}">
    <Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" Text="Hello" />
</ContentPage>
```

`DynamicResource` (not `StaticResource`) is what makes live switching work — it re-evaluates when the dictionary changes.

### 5. Settings page picker

```xml
<Picker x:Name="ThemePicker"
        ItemsSource="{Binding Themes}"
        SelectedIndexChanged="OnThemeChanged" />
```

```csharp
void OnThemeChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    if (ThemePicker.SelectedItem is string theme)
        ThemeManager.ApplyTheme(theme);
}
```

**Key points:**
- Same resource keys across all three dictionaries ensures seamless swapping.
- `DynamicResource` bindings update immediately when dictionaries are replaced.
- `Preferences` persists the choice across app restarts.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to set up themed styling in a .NET MAUI app with three themes (light, dark, high-contrast) that can be switched at runtime from a settings page. This is a knowledge/guidance question, not a code-change task in an existing repo. I'll provide a concise but complete answer.
